executive.strengthHeadline: Your strength in Technology & Operations is a genuine asset
executive.introBody: You're building real foundations across all six domains. The work ahead is converting initial structures into formal, documented, owned processes.

domains.name: Technology & Operations
domains.iso: Clause 8 (Operation), Annex A.6 (AI System Life Cycle), A.9 (Use of AI Systems)

actions.title: Implement human oversight proportional to risk.
actions.body: Not every AI output needs human review, but high-stakes decisions do. Define clear criteria for oversight requirements.
actions.quickWin: Classify your AI use cases by risk level and define the oversight requirement for each.

actions.title: Build lifecycle management into AI operations.
actions.body: AI systems aren't "deploy and forget." They need monitoring, revalidation, updates, and eventually retirement.
actions.quickWin: Schedule the first review of your longest-running AI system.

actions.title: Create a simple "approved tools" list.
actions.body: You don't need to block AI — you need to guide it. Define which AI tools are approved, for what purposes, and with what guardrails.
actions.quickWin: Publish an "Approved AI Tools" list with 2–3 tools and basic usage guidelines.

domains.name: People & Culture
domains.iso: Clause 7 (Support), Annex A.4 (Resources for AI Systems)

actions.title: Move from ad hoc learning to a structured AI competency framework.
actions.body: Define what "AI-literate" means for each role in your organization. Not everyone needs the same depth.
actions.quickWin: Define AI competency expectations for 3 key roles in your organization.

actions.title: Invest in change management for AI adoption.
actions.body: Technical readiness without cultural readiness fails. Address fears, communicate transparently about AI's role.
actions.quickWin: Run an anonymous survey: "How do you feel about AI in our workplace?" Use results to tailor communication.

actions.title: Identify your AI champions.
actions.body: There are people in your organization already curious about AI. Find them, support them, and let them lead by example.
actions.quickWin: Identify 2–3 AI-curious employees and give them time and license to explore AI tools relevant to their roles.

domains.name: Data Readiness
domains.iso: Clause 8 (Operation), Annex A.7 (Data for AI Systems)

actions.title: Establish data quality standards with AI use cases in mind.
actions.body: Define what "good enough for AI" means for your key datasets: completeness, accuracy, timeliness, consistency.
actions.quickWin: Define data quality thresholds for one dataset you plan to use with AI.

actions.title: Implement data provenance and lineage tracking for AI-critical data.
actions.body: As you scale AI, you need to trace data from source to decision. This supports transparency, debugging, and regulatory compliance.
actions.quickWin: Document the complete data lineage for your most important AI system.

actions.title: Clean and organize your most valuable business data first.
actions.body: You don't need perfect data everywhere. Identify the 2–3 datasets most relevant to your business and focus your governance efforts there.
actions.quickWin: Pick your most important dataset and address its most obvious quality issues.

actions.title: Formalize AI governance ownership.
actions.body: Without clear ownership, AI governance becomes everyone's concern and no one's responsibility. Assign a specific person or function.
actions.quickWin: Designate an AI governance lead with a defined mandate, even if it's a partial role.

actions.title: Connect your AI strategy to measurable business outcomes.
actions.body: You have the structure — now ensure it drives results. Define 3–5 KPIs that link AI initiatives to business value.
actions.quickWin: Identify one AI initiative and define how you'll measure its business impact in the next quarter.

actions.title: Move from informal AI conversations to a documented position.
actions.body: A one-page "AI Intent Document" stating what AI means for your business, what you will and won't use it for, and who makes AI decisions is a powerful starting point.
actions.quickWin: Draft a 1-page AI Intent Document and share it with your team.

actions.title: Establish a formal AI impact assessment process.
actions.body: Before deploying AI in decisions that affect people, run a structured assessment: who's affected, what could go wrong, how do you detect it, what's the fallback?
actions.quickWin: Create an AI impact assessment template and pilot it on your next AI deployment.

actions.title: Build a simple incident response playbook for AI failures.
actions.body: When (not if) an AI system produces a bad outcome, your team should know: who to call, what to document, how to communicate with affected parties.
actions.quickWin: Draft a 1-page AI incident response card for your most-used AI system.

actions.title: Conduct a simple AI risk brainstorm.
actions.body: Gather key staff for 90 minutes. For each AI tool you use, ask: "What's the worst that could happen? What would we do?" Document the answers.
actions.quickWin: Run the risk brainstorm session and document the output in a shared document.

domains.name: Ethics, Transparency & Trust
domains.iso: Clause 9, Annex A.8 (Information for Interested Parties), A.10 (Third-Party)

actions.title: Assess your third-party AI supply chain.
actions.body: Your AI risk extends to every vendor whose AI you use. What data do they access? How do their models work?
actions.quickWin: Review the terms of service and data handling practices of your top 3 AI vendors.

actions.title: Implement AI performance monitoring with ethical indicators.
actions.body: Track not just whether AI works, but whether it works fairly. Bias metrics and stakeholder satisfaction measurements should complement your operational KPIs.
actions.quickWin: Add one fairness metric to your monitoring dashboard for your highest-impact AI system.

actions.title: Write a simple "Our approach to AI" statement.
actions.body: Something your customers and employees can read. Not legal jargon — plain language about how you use AI.
actions.quickWin: Draft a 1-paragraph "How We Use AI" statement for your website or employee handbook.

recommendation.headline: Implement human oversight proportional to risk.
recommendation.quickWin: Classify your AI use cases by risk level and define the oversight requirement for each.

recommendation.headline: Move from ad hoc learning to a structured AI competency framework.
recommendation.quickWin: Define AI competency expectations for 3 key roles in your organization.

recommendation.headline: Establish data quality standards with AI use cases in mind.
recommendation.quickWin: Define data quality thresholds for one dataset you plan to use with AI.

eligible.name: ESSOR — Sous-volet 1B (Diagnostic numérique)
eligible.summary: Non-repayable Quebec grant for digital diagnostics — open to all sectors.

eligible.name: ESSOR — Sous-volet 1C (Mise en œuvre)
eligible.summary: Quebec funding to implement your digital plan — open to all sectors.

eligible.name: NRC IRAP (Industrial Research Assistance Program)
eligible.summary: Federal non-repayable funding for innovative AI SME projects.

eligible.name: Regional AI Initiative (RAII)
eligible.summary: Federal funding for AI commercialization and adoption projects.

eligible.name: SR&ED (Scientific Research & Experimental Development)
eligible.summary: Federal tax credit for R&D activities including AI development.

eligible.name: Quebec CRIC (Crédit d'impôt R-D, innovation et précommercialisation)
eligible.summary: Quebec provincial innovation tax credit, distinct from the older C3i.

investigate.name: CDAE-IA (Crédit d'impôt — affaires électroniques + IA)
investigate.summary: Quebec AI salary tax credit for IT/software firms with significant AI integration.
investigate.note: Requires significant AI integration in e-business activities, plus 75%+ revenue from IT and 50%+ from software development. As your AI use deepens, this credit may apply.

investigate.name: AI Compute Access Fund
investigate.summary: Federal subsidized access to high-performance AI computing.
investigate.note: Most relevant for organizations with active AI development requiring significant compute resources. Currently closed; monitor for the next call.

loi25.verdictBody: Awareness without operational readiness. The work is converting "we know we should" into "we have a documented procedure and a named owner."

loi25Questions.title: What data is actually necessary?
loi25Questions.responseText: We collect whatever data is available; there is no documented constraint on what is necessary.
loi25Questions.nextStep: For your most-used AI tool: list the data fields, decide which are actually necessary, and ask the vendor how to disable the rest.

loi25Questions.title: Make the named person publicly findable.
loi25Questions.responseText: A person has been named internally, but their identity is not published on our website or in our privacy policy.
loi25Questions.nextStep: Add the name and contact email to your website privacy policy and your email signature template.

loi25Questions.title: CAI reporting playbook.
loi25Questions.responseText: We have a general incident process but it does not specifically address Loi 25's "risk of serious harm" assessment or CAI notification timing.
loi25Questions.nextStep: Schedule a 30-minute tabletop with the named person + 2 staff. Document where the playbook broke down.

loi25Questions.title: Tighten reasons-on-demand response time.
loi25Questions.responseText: We could produce the explanation within a few business days for some systems but not all.
loi25Questions.nextStep: For the slowest-to-explain system: build a 1-page template that captures the recurring fields. Future requests become fill-in-the-blank.

loi25Questions.title: Where does your data actually live?
loi25Questions.responseText: We have not considered where our AI vendors process or store data.
loi25Questions.nextStep: List your top 10 AI tools and check the data residency clause in each vendor's terms. Note which ones store/process data outside Quebec.

loi25Questions.title: Keep the register current.
loi25Questions.responseText: We have a register, but it is incomplete or not regularly updated.
loi25Questions.nextStep: Block a recurring monthly 30-minute calendar slot for the named person to update the register.
